trajectory.model_output:
<python_code>
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# First, open the gripper before grasping
open_gripper()

# Get the grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Approach and grasp the handle with a z_approach to ensure safe approach
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Final grasp pose without approach offset

# Close the gripper to grasp the nut
close_gripper()

# Now, get the pose of the white hollow center of the brown square nut to compute the relative transform
# This will help us align the nut correctly during insertion
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute the relative rigid transform from handle to center (in object frame)
# This transform will be used to align the nut properly when inserting onto the block
relative_position_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position

# Convert quaternions to rotation objects
rot_handle = R.from_quat(np.roll(handle_quaternion_wxyz, -1))  # Convert WXYZ -> XYZW
rot_center = R.from_quat(np.roll(center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Relative rotation from handle frame to center frame
relative_rotation_handle_to_center = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()

# Now, get the pose of the brown square block (target insertion point)
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Desired final center pose of the nut when inserted: same as block's pose
final_center_position = block_position
final_center_rotation = R.from_quat(np.roll(block_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Now compute the desired handle pose using the inverse relative transform
# Desired handle position = final center position - (final center rotation @ relative_position_handle_to_center)
desired_handle_position = final_center_position - final_center_rotation.apply(relative_position_handle_to_center)

# Desired handle rotation = final center rotation * relative_rotation_handle_to_center.inverse()
desired_handle_rotation = final_center_rotation * relative_rotation_handle_to_center.inv()
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = R.as_quat(desired_handle_rotation)  # Returns XYZW
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = np.roll(desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, 1)  # Convert XYZW -> WXYZ

# Move above the insertion point with z_approach for precision
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)

# Perform the final insertion motion
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Optionally retract slightly after insertion
retract_position = desired_handle_position + np.array([0, 0, 0.05])
goto_pose(retract_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Open gripper to release the nut
open_gripper()

# Return to home joint configuration
goto_home_joint_position()
</python_code>

import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# First, open the gripper before grasping
open_gripper()

# Get the grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Approach and grasp the handle with a z_approach to ensure safe approach
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Final grasp pose without approach offset

# Close the gripper to grasp the nut
close_gripper()

# Now, get the pose of the white hollow center of the brown square nut to compute the relative transform
# This will help us align the nut correctly during insertion
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute the relative rigid transform from handle to center (in object frame)
# This transform will be used to align the nut properly when inserting onto the block
relative_position_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position

# Convert quaternions to rotation objects
rot_handle = R.from_quat(np.roll(handle_quaternion_wxyz, -1))  # Convert WXYZ -> XYZW
rot_center = R.from_quat(np.roll(center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Relative rotation from handle frame to center frame
relative_rotation_handle_to_center = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()

# Now, get the pose of the brown square block (target insertion point)
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Desired final center pose of the nut when inserted: same as block's pose
final_center_position = block_position
final_center_rotation = R.from_quat(np.roll(block_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Now compute the desired handle pose using the inverse relative transform
# Desired handle position = final center position - (final center rotation @ relative_position_handle_to_center)
desired_handle_position = final_center_position - final_center_rotation.apply(relative_position_handle_to_center)

# Desired handle rotation = final center rotation * relative_rotation_handle_to_center.inverse()
desired_handle_rotation = final_center_rotation * relative_rotation_handle_to_center.inv()
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = R.as_quat(desired_handle_rotation)  # Returns XYZW
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = np.roll(desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, 1)  # Convert XYZW -> WXYZ

# Move above the insertion point with z_approach for precision
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)

# Perform the final insertion motion
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Optionally retract slightly after insertion
retract_position = desired_handle_position + np.array([0, 0, 0.05])
goto_pose(retract_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Open gripper to release the nut
open_gripper()

# Return to home joint configuration
goto_home_joint_position()

trajectory.model_output:
<python_code>
# The task was not fully completed, likely due to imprecise alignment during insertion.
# Let's retry the insertion with a more conservative approach: additional alignment checks and slower, precise motions.

# First, re-approach the handle grasp in case the object shifted
open_gripper()
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)
close_gripper()

# Re-query the center of the nut and the block to ensure up-to-date poses
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Recompute relative transform from handle to center
relative_position_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position
rot_handle = R.from_quat(np.roll(handle_quaternion_wxyz, -1))
rot_center = R.from_quat(np.roll(center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))
relative_rotation_handle_to_center = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()

# Desired final center pose is aligned with the block
final_center_position = block_position
final_center_rotation = R.from_quat(np.roll(block_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Compute desired handle pose after insertion
desired_handle_position = final_center_position - final_center_rotation.apply(relative_position_handle_to_center)
desired_handle_rotation = final_center_rotation * relative_rotation_handle_to_center.inv()
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = np.roll(R.as_quat(desired_handle_rotation), 1)  # To WXYZ

# Perform precise insertion with larger approach distance for stability
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.12)
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.02)  # Fine approach
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Final insertion

# Hold briefly to ensure physical settling
import time
time.sleep(1)

# Retract slowly to avoid dragging
retract_position = desired_handle_position + np.array([0, 0, 0.05])
goto_pose(retract_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Return to home position
goto_home_joint_position()
</python_code>

tool_infos.tool_input:
# The task was not fully completed, likely due to imprecise alignment during insertion.
# Let's retry the insertion with a more conservative approach: additional alignment checks and slower, precise motions.

# First, re-approach the handle grasp in case the object shifted
open_gripper()
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)
close_gripper()

# Re-query the center of the nut and the block to ensure up-to-date poses
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Recompute relative transform from handle to center
relative_position_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position
rot_handle = R.from_quat(np.roll(handle_quaternion_wxyz, -1))
rot_center = R.from_quat(np.roll(center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))
relative_rotation_handle_to_center = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()

# Desired final center pose is aligned with the block
final_center_position = block_position
final_center_rotation = R.from_quat(np.roll(block_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Compute desired handle pose after insertion
desired_handle_position = final_center_position - final_center_rotation.apply(relative_position_handle_to_center)
desired_handle_rotation = final_center_rotation * relative_rotation_handle_to_center.inv()
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = np.roll(R.as_quat(desired_handle_rotation), 1)  # To WXYZ

# Perform precise insertion with larger approach distance for stability
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.12)
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.02)  # Fine approach
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Final insertion

# Hold briefly to ensure physical settling
import time
time.sleep(1)

# Retract slowly to avoid dragging
retract_position = desired_handle_position + np.array([0, 0, 0.05])
goto_pose(retract_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Return to home position
goto_home_joint_position()
